formulas.name: Law of Demand
formulas.formula: Inverse relationship between price and quantity demanded
formulas.notes: A decrease in price leads to an increase in quantity demanded, and vice versa.

formulas.name: Law of Supply
formulas.formula: Direct relationship between price and quantity supplied
formulas.notes: An increase in price leads to an increase in quantity supplied, and vice versa.

formulas.name: Equilibrium Price and Quantity
formulas.formula: Where the demand curve intersects the supply curve
formulas.notes: The equilibrium price and quantity are where the quantity demanded equals the quantity supplied.

formulas.name: Consumer's Budget Constraint
formulas.formula: Total income (Y) = price of good 1 (P1) * quantity of good 1 (Q1) + price of good 2 (P2) * quantity of good 2 (Q2)
formulas.variables: Total income (Y), price of good 1 (P1), quantity of good 1 (Q1), price of good 2 (P2), quantity of good 2 (Q2)
formulas.notes: A graphical representation of the consumer's budget constraint is a straight line with a negative slope.

formulas.name: Opportunity Cost
formulas.formula: The value of the next best alternative foregone when a choice is made
formulas.notes: Opportunity cost is a fundamental concept in microeconomics that helps in decision-making.

formulas.name: Marginal Benefit (MB) and Marginal Cost (MC)
formulas.formula: MB = Change in Total Benefit / Change in Quantity, MC = Change in Total Cost / Change in Quantity
formulas.notes: MB and MC are used to determine the optimal quantity and make decisions in microeconomics.
